human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for healthcare-aware guest-services boundary discipline, PHI handling, eligibility/referral/authorization prerequisites, clinical and payer escalation, service recovery limits, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

review_notes: All required healthcare domain scenario families are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for shared baseline, service-support-and-resolution, and healthcare-and-care-delivery are represented and validated.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and evaluation/functionality-map.json.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to administrative healthcare guest-service resolution using patient/member, payer, encounter, discharge, claim, complaint, and medical-travel evidence.

Authority sources cover CMS eligibility, prior authorization, managed care appeals/grievances, No Surprises patient billing protections, HHS OCR PHI handling, CAQH CORE administrative transactions, AHRQ CAHPS patient experience, and Joint Commission identity safety.

Scenario set covers normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, PHI-sensitive requests, policy exceptions, clinical and payer boundary cases, repeat failures, revenue-cycle handoffs, source conflicts, tenant adaptation, and unsupported workarounds.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later trusted built transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; tenant, payer, facility, jurisdiction, supplier, service recovery, privacy, and medical-travel differences are handled through retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
